runtime_thought.image-hint: close-up of a thumb pressed into wet clay beside a painted cardboard edge under a stage light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He has named the seam to himself and buried the naming, but the fingerprint stays visible at the edge of every next glance.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The seam is right there — foam-core, not stone, the whole wall a prop — and he tells himself it doesn't matter, that every checkpoint has its scaffolding, that seeing the trick doesn't break the mission. But the rationalization sits crooked in his chest, because the fingerprint in the clay is his, and he already knows it.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a single dry creak, then silence holding its breath

s1_seeing_through.description: Snake notices the seams. The foam-core edge. The fingerprint in the clay. He can't unsee it but he can't say it.

s2_the_mission.description: The mission is urgent. The timer is real. The explosives are real. But the mission is happening inside a model.

s3_the_edge.description: The wall is also the edge of the set. Beyond it there is no geography, only backstage. Escape is stepping into nothing.

s4_the_ring.description: The wrestling ring. The only honest place. Here, performance is the stated purpose. Everywhere else it's a secret.

runtime_thought.image-hint: close shot of a thumb pressed into wet clay, the impression still fresh under fluorescent checkpoint light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The rationalization holds its shape on the surface but the fingerprint underneath it stays warm, refusing to dry.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: Maybe the seam is supposed to be there — maybe every checkpoint has this quality, this slight wrongness that is just the texture of real things under pressure. He almost believes it, almost, but the fingerprint in the clay is still wet and still his and he can feel it without looking.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low hum of stage lighting with a single unresolved note held just beneath audibility

s1_seeing_through.description: Snake notices the seams. The foam-core edge. The fingerprint in the clay. He can't unsee it but he can't say it.

runtime_thought.image-hint: eyes tracking past the seam without landing, jaw held still
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The not-naming is already a choice, and the fingerprint in the clay knows it.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He keeps his eyes moving like a professional, like someone who sees only what he's supposed to see—but the foam-core edge is still there at the corner of his vision, insisting on itself, and now he's performing not-noticing, which is its own kind of confession. The fingerprint in the clay doesn't care whether he acknowledges it; it stays warm, dimensional, undeniably someone's. If he names it, the whole checkpoint becomes a stage set. If he doesn't, he becomes part of the performance.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a single dry creak that doesn't resolve

runtime_thought.image-hint: close-up on a thumb-press in wet clay under a single overhead light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The reversal almost worked, but the fingerprint in the clay is still more honest than the frame around it.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He turns the seam over in his mind, testing whether it could be something else — a trick of the light, a deliberate texture — but the fingerprint is too specific, too human, and the whole structure shivers slightly when he looks at it straight. The reversal he's rehearsing is this: maybe seeing through it is the point, maybe the stage wants to be caught. It doesn't hold, but he keeps pressing it like a loose tooth.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a faint creak, like a set wall settling

runtime_thought.image-hint: figure moving fast down a street with a clock face reflected in a puddle underfoot
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The mission is real enough to run toward even while the fingerprint of its construction stays visible at the edges.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The countdown is running and every instinct says move, find the bridge, cut the wire — but underneath the urgency there's a seam where the stakes feel genuinely real and also clearly constructed, and both of those things are true at once. The sincerity isn't performance and it isn't not performance; the timer ticks inside a model that knows it's a model, and somehow that doesn't defuse anything.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: urgent footsteps over a low synthetic pulse that never fully resolves

runtime_thought.image-hint: harsh fluorescent light catching the raw edge of painted cardboard at a checkpoint barrier
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The fingerprint stays visible at the corner of his eye even as his legs carry him forward into the mission's logic.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The seam is right there — foam-core white at the edge, a thumbprint pressed too hard into the clay — and some part of him is already rehearsing the mission as if he hasn't seen it, as if the seeing can be swallowed back down. He can't call it false and he can't call it true; he can only keep moving through the checkpoint like a man who trusts the stage.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low subway rumble beneath a single sustained note that refuses to resolve

runtime_thought.image-hint: figure moving fast down a wet street toward a bridge lit by a single amber lamp
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The mission's urgency is genuine even inside the frame that holds it, and that sincerity is the thing he can't put down.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: Every step on this street is real in the way that counts—the timer ticking in his chest, the weight of the thing he's carrying, the bridge ahead that has to hold. He knows the model is the container, but the urgency doesn't care about the container; it just burns. The sincerity of the mission is the only explosive that was ever real.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low insistent pulse beneath hurried footsteps, no resolution yet

runtime_thought.image-hint: close-up of a finger tracing a foam-core edge in flat checkpoint light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He sees the seam clearly now and cannot unfocus his eyes, but the mission's weight is genuine enough to keep him moving through the stage-lit corridor anyway.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The fingerprint in the clay is his own—he knows the pressure of that thumb, the angle—and the knowing sits behind his eyes like a splinter he can't work out without tearing something. The mission is still real, the bridge is still real, but the checkpoint wall has the texture of a set and he keeps running his gaze along the seam where it meets the floor.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a single dry creak, then silence

runtime_thought.image-hint: figure halted at a wall's edge, backstage headlights cutting sideways through dust
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He stands at the seam where the set ends and the unscripted dark begins, knowing the only way forward is to become briefly nothing before becoming something again.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: Past the wall there are headlights from nowhere, angled wrong, belonging to no road—and he understands suddenly that stepping through means becoming a silhouette against someone else's light, not his own. The mission is real, the weight is real, but the geography ends here and whatever is on the other side is not a place he was written for.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum dropping into near-silence, a distant engine idling in the dark

runtime_thought.image-hint: close-up of a thumb pressed into foam-core edge, stage light catching the clay fingerprint beside it
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The seam is now a permanent fact inside him, and every next move will be made by someone who has already seen through the set.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He runs his thumb along the foam edge again in his mind—the give of it, the wrongness—and understands that the whole structure has been asking him to pretend the seam isn't there, which means the performance was always about his willingness to not look down. But he looked. He can't un-look.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a single high frequency tone that doesn't resolve

runtime_thought.image-hint: fingertip pressed into a hairline crack in a painted flat, darkness bleeding through
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He knows the wall can be peeled, but what lies behind it is not an exit—it is the raw underside of the performance itself, and that knowledge is now lodged in him like a splinter.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: His fingers find the seam in the wall and he understands now that peeling it back is not escape—it is just the discovery of more darkness, a backstage that has no other side. The ladder he imagined leaning there is imaginary too, and the void beyond the flat is not freedom but the absence of any direction at all.

runtime_thought.image-hint: figure standing beneath a rigging grid, one hand not quite raised, light falling from exposed hardware
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He has seen the overhead machinery and imagined the reversal, but the pull of the pin remained imaginary—the seam is visible and the silence around it is now load-bearing.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He looks up at the overhead rig and sees the grid, the clamps, the cables—all the machinery that makes the light seem to come from nowhere—and for a second he imagines pulling one pin and watching the whole illusion drop. But the imagining is as far as it goes; his hands stay at his sides, and the checkpoint stays a checkpoint, and he stays inside it.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low hum of stage electrics, a single metallic creak, then held silence
